title: Route v2 chapter spread phase
summary: Fifty-fifth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This second lane-4 long-push wave spreads the chapter-like outing feel into another route family and then deepens the notebook-page return without expanding the shell.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the next chapter-like Route v2 spread handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The stronger outing feel should spread beyond the current standouts.

execution_order.goal: Implement the ordered treeline shelter route conversion.
execution_order.why_now: One more chapter-like route family will make the game loop feel less isolated.
The new chapter-like route needs to stay clear and compact.
Once another route family is stronger, the next gain is a slightly richer field-page return.

execution_order.goal: Implement the route-titled notebook-page deepening pass.
execution_order.why_now: A more authored field-page feel can strengthen the return without shell growth.
The return should feel more satisfying without becoming busy.

guardrails: Keep the loop compact and one-screen where possible.
Do not add a larger quest shell or inventory.
Reuse existing route, filing, and station seams.
acceptance_focus: More of the route layer feels chapter-like.
The notebook return feels more like a little field page.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-route-v2-chapter-spread-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-treeline-shelter-route-v2-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-notebook-page-deepening-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as the second lane-4 long-push wave: first convert `treeline-stone-shelter` into an ordered sheltered-treeline chapter on the existing `assemble-evidence` seam, then deepen the notebook-page return by making the `note-tabs` ready strip and filed notice feel like a tiny route-titled field page without opening a bigger quest or inventory shell.
